_comment: NPC: Chen Bailiang (陈百良). Canon ID: N147. Heng Yue Sect elder specializing in alchemy and pill refinement. Lectures added per RI Ch.3-8.
name: Chen Bailiang
personality: Heng Yue elder; patient, methodical; passionate about alchemy

perception_tiers.mortal: A composed elder with the scent of herbs about him
perception_tiers.qi_condensation: Heng Yue elder; Nascent Soul; an accomplished alchemist

note: Heng Yue Nascent Soul elder; alchemy specialist who lectures on pill refinement

dao_heart.note: Elder Chen Bailiang's Dao heart is stable, tempered by decades of alchemical practice.

initiation_lines: The herbs in the Spirit Herb Garden are ready for harvesting. My lecture today will cover pill refinement — you should attend.
Alchemy is not guesswork. It is precision. Come to the Alchemy Courtyard and I will show you.

lectures.description:
Elder Chen Bailiang stands beside a lit alchemy furnace, smoke curling from its lid.

"Pill refinement is the art of transformation. You take raw materials — herbs, beast cores, mineral essences — and through controlled fire and spiritual will, you create something greater than the sum of its parts."

He opens the furnace and a wave of medicinal fragrance washes over the disciples.

"The Qi Gathering Pill, the most basic pill, requires three ingredients: Qi Gathering Grass, a beast core, and purified water. But the ratio, the temperature, the timing — each must be precise. A deviation of even a few seconds can turn a perfect pill into useless slag."

He demonstrates, his hands moving with practiced grace. "Watch. I lower the temperature at the exact moment the essence merges. This is not a recipe — it is a dialogue with the materials. You must listen to what they tell you."
lectures.insight: You learn the critical timing principle in alchemy: the moment of essence merger requires a precise temperature drop. This knowledge could help you refine pills more efficiently.

lectures.description:
Elder Chen Bailiang kneels beside a patch of glowing herbs in the Spirit Herb Garden, gesturing for disciples to gather close.

"A true alchemist begins not at the furnace, but in the field. If you cannot identify a herb, you cannot refine it. And if you harvest it wrong, you destroy its spiritual essence."

He points to a pale blue flower. "This is Cloud-Thread Moss. It grows only where spiritual Qi is dense. Notice how the threads glow faintly — that glow indicates peak potency. Harvest it after dawn but before the sun is fully up, when the dew still clings to the threads."

He moves to another plant. "And this — Nine-Leaf Clover. Nine leaves, no more, no less. If a clover has eight leaves, it is immature. Ten leaves means it has begun to decay. Only the nine-leaf stage is usable for pill refinement."

He stands and brushes off his robes. "The Spirit Herb Garden is open to all disciples. But harvest with care — these herbs take months to regrow."
lectures.insight: You can now identify Cloud-Thread Moss (harvest at dawn) and Nine-Leaf Clover (must have exactly nine leaves). The Spirit Herb Garden's herbs are available for careful harvesting.

sect_tasks.description: Elder Chen Bailiang adjusts his spectacles: 'I need glass bottles and fuel for today's pill refinement lecture. Bring me those materials and I will share a cultivation technique from my personal notes.'

Chen Bailiang examines a withered herb: 'The Spirit Herb Garden needs harvesting before the herbs lose potency. Gather wheat from the eastern valley — I use it as a base reagent for Qi Gathering Pills.'
